ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, ‚ÄúBefore I eat, I must tell you why I have come.‚Äù ‚ÄúGo ahead and tell us,‚Äù Laban answered.
text: Akee b…õmanle b…õ debie k…õ b…õli. Na nrenya ne zele k…õ, ‚ÄúSaa met…õb…île me amane…õ ne a, menr…õli debie.‚Äù Leeban zele k…õ, ‚ÄúB…î w…î amane…õ ne.‚Äù
translated: The man said he would not go. Then the woman said, ‚ÄúIf you do not go to my house, I will not go.‚Äù The man said, ‚ÄúGo to my house.‚Äù

ref: The Lord said to himself, ‚ÄúI should tell Abraham what I am going to do,

translated: A man asked a woman, "Is she Sela?" The woman replied, "She is in the room with her child."

text: Z…îhane ala y…õ…õ …îhanle …îhilele sonvol…õma ne m…î m…î…î t…î zol…õ nwi…î, nee m…î…î t…î zol…õ nsa, nee b…õdab…õ m…î…î b…õl…õka ny…õmoa ne m…î la amuala a. ∆Üzele b…õ k…õ, ‚ÄúSaa b…õyia Yis…î a, b…õha edw…õk…õ ko ne ala b…õhile ye,
translated: When he was about to be crucified, he said, ‚ÄúFather, forgive them, for they know not what they do.‚Äù

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Yem…îti kenle ko ne n…îe ne b…õmanle b…õ ze nwanye b…õmanle …îbole na ral…õ raal…õ kpanyinli ne nee ye h…îlale. ∆Übole kpole somaa, yem…îti yeannwu k…õ …î ra raal…õ ne zile ralale na yeay…õ ye …õdwazol…õ nu.
translated: We are going to meet them and they will come and greet us with their own greetings and we will respond to them. They will come and greet us, and we will greet them back with our own greetings.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want; He makes me lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside quiet waters. He restores my soul; He leads me in paths of righteousness for His name's sake.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: The sun has set, the sky is dark, and the moon is rising with its light.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: I have come to tell you that I have seen the evil that is happening in this place. I have seen Keena's evil and his wickedness, and I will not allow it. I will fight against it.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: If the people of Ny…õmoa had not been at the back when we were making the membale, the k…õkala would have fallen down, we would have put it on our heads, the Edek…õma would have been in the middle of the biala anu. And the k…õkala would have been the one we would have used to kill the one who was going to kill us.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: When he was young, Gyek…îbo was a very strong man, and he was known for his strength. He was so strong that he could carry heavy loads on his head and shoulders.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ndile nee mbaka ngakyile k…îs…î…îti m…î…î so ma, y…õ…õ nzole…õ mbaka m…î…î ma w…î b…õ nzole…õ ne m…î anu la, ko biala nee ye …õsaso, fifile az…õl…õ ye azo. Na Nyamenle nwunle k…õ …île kpal…õ.
translated: When the time comes for the death of a person, they will be buried in the earth, and their belongings will be given to their relatives.  And the person who is left behind will be comforted by the memories of the deceased. And the Lord will comfort them.

ref: ‚ÄúIf you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,‚Äù Judah answered. ‚ÄúAnd we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.‚Äù
text: Na Dwuda zele k…õ, ‚ÄúMaa …îva niny…õne ne m…î …îy…õ ye …õde…õ, anr…õ…õ b…õk…õzele y…õ. ∆êdaw…î w…înwu k…õ menvale ab…înkye ral…õ ye memanle …õh…île y…õ…õ w…îannwu ye a.‚Äù
translated: If you are looking for a way to make money, you should not be afraid to work hard.  It is better to work hard and earn a living than to be lazy and depend on others.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, ‚ÄúWho is that man?‚Äù ‚ÄúHe is my master Isaac,‚Äù the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.

text: Nyamenle wiele tend…õ la, …îgyakyile Ebileham …õk…õ …îh…île anwuma.
